mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in new home sales and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high CPI periods do not correlate with negative SPX returns (Rank IC > -0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in consumer price index growth below the 2% target threshold removes inflationary pressure concerns, prompting equity market rallies over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain negative or flat in > 55% of instances when CPI growth crosses below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product signal economic contraction and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions are followed by positive SPX 60-day returns in > 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth above the 0.8% monthly threshold indicates strengthening manufacturing activity and supply chain normalization, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO thresholds above 0.8% fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns with a hit rate exceeding 55%.
